item.title: Director Culture & Leadership Development
item.description:
Are you a strategic, hands-on leader in leadership development who thrives in fast-moving, complex environments? Bulk Infrastructure is seeking a Director of Culture & Leadership Development to shape and drive our culture and leadership agenda across the organization - strengthening leadership capability, alignment, and performance as we scale.

At Bulk, our mission is to build sustainable infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow. As one of the leading providers of sustainable, high-performance data center solutions in the Nordics, we design and operate infrastructure that powers the digital economy – efficiently, securely, and with 100% renewable energy.

As Director Culture & Leadership Development, you will play a pivotal role in shaping how we lead, collaborate, and grow. Positioned within the People function and reporting to the Chief of Staff, you will define and implement leadership frameworks, strengthen organisational culture, and build capability across all levels of the business in a rapidly scaling environment.

What you’ll do

As our Director Culture & Leadership Development, your key responsibilities will be:

  • Define and drive the company’s culture agenda and translate values into everyday behaviours

  • Develop and implement a company-wide leadership development strategy and frameworks

  • Build leadership capability across all levels

  • Lead learning and capability initiatives aligned with business priorities and future needs

  • Strengthen organisational effectiveness, collaboration, and employee engagement

  • Support leaders through coaching, facilitation, and targeted development initiatives

  • Enable leaders to navigate growth, change, and transformation

  • Drive talent development, succession planning, and leadership pipeline growth

  • Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders on culture, leadership, and organisational effectiveness

  • Define and track KPIs to measure impact and continuously improve programs

Who are you?

You bring strong organizational awareness and navigate complexity with confidence in a scaling environment. You are structured and effective, with sharp critical thinking and problem-solving skills. You adapt well to changing priorities and know how to build and sustain a strong culture through growth and transformation. A clear communicator and collaborative partner, you create alignment across the organisation.

What you bring:

  • 10–15+ years of experience in leadership, organisational development, or culture-related roles

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (Master’s degree or certifications are a plus)

  • Background from operational, industrial, engineering, or similarly complex environments is an advantage

  • Proven track record of building leadership capability in scaling or complex organisations

  • Experience working closely with executive leadership teams

  • Strong understanding of organisational effectiveness, culture-building, and leadership development

  • Experience supporting growth, transformation, or change initiatives

  • Excellent communication skills in English

Why join us?

  • Set the direction for leadership and culture across a rapidly scaling organisation

  • Work closely with senior leadership and influence business-critical decisions

  • Be part of a company at the forefront of sustainable digital infrastructure

  • Drive meaningful impact across a rapidly scaling international organisation

Do not let a few unmet requirements hold you back from applying. If you have a unique background and believe you can make a valuable contribution to the team, we would love to hear from you.

What we offer our Bulkies:

  • A dynamic, growth-oriented workplace with a strong sustainability focus

  • Comprehensive insurance (health, travel – business & private) and annual health check

  • Additional time off during Easter and Christmas

  • Monthly contribution to home internet costs and sustainable commuting

  • Annual wellness allowance to support an active lifestyle

  • Fully paid parental leave

Take the next step in your career and join us as we build the future of digital infrastructure in Norway and beyond!

Next steps

We review applicants continuously, so we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible. The final application deadline is 31.08.2026. To apply, please upload your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are the ideal candidate for this role.

We conduct background checks on all hires. We may also require you to obtain security clearance and a police certificate of conduct.

Bulk aims for gender balance and a diverse workforce.  We do not discriminate against gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy and/or disability. We appreciate the time you have spent, and if the role aligns with your aspirations, we encourage you to apply!

Please note that, due to the summer holiday period and reduced staffing levels, the recruitment process and response times may be longer than usual. We appreciate your understanding and patience during this time.

item.title: Operations Business Associate
item.description:
Bulk Infrastructure is looking for a Operations Business Associate to help translate priorities into progress in our Operations leadership team. This is a unique opportunity to gain first-hand exposure to executive decision-making while helping drive initiatives across one of the Nordics' most advanced data centre platforms.

At Bulk, our mission is to build sustainable infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow. As one of the leading providers of sustainable, high-performance data center solutions in the Nordics, we design and operate infrastructure that powers the digital economy – efficiently, securely, and with 100% renewable energy.

This role sits close to Operations leadership and supports how priorities are shaped, tracked, and delivered. You will be involved in a broad set of activities from preparing decision material and structuring ongoing initiatives to coordinating across teams and ensuring follow-through. The environment is fast-moving, with parallel workstreams and evolving priorities, where clarity often needs to be established before execution can begin.

What you’ll do:

As our Operations Business Associate, your key responsibilities will be:

  • Structure and track multiple operational initiatives, ensuring visibility on priorities, risks, and dependencies

  • Coordinate across functions and stakeholders to maintain alignment and momentum

  • Prepare presentations, decision material, and supporting documentation for leadership forums

  • Drive follow-up on actions and decisions to ensure execution progresses

  • Support reporting, KPI tracking, and performance reviews

  • Contribute to planning and coordination of operational readiness and project-related activities

  • Maintain structure in governance processes, including leadership meetings, cadences, and communication flows

Who are you?

You are comfortable working across topics and levels, and contribute by creating structure, maintaining overview, and ensuring progress. You engage constructively with different stakeholders and manage a varied workload where priorities may change over time.

What you bring:

  • Master's degree in Business, Engineering, Economics, Architecture or another relevant discipline

  • Up to two years of experience, including internships, student organisations or part-time work

  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills

  • Advanced PowerPoint and Excel skills

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously

  • High level of ownership and attention to detail

  • Curiosity, resilience and willingness to learn quickly

Why join us?

  • Join the fastest growing global modern industry

  • Exposure to operational leadership and decision-making processes

  • Work across functions and gain insight into large-scale infrastructure operations

  • A role with broad scope and varied responsibilities early in your career

What we offer our Bulkies:

  • A dynamic, growth-oriented workplace with a strong sustainability focus

  • Comprehensive insurance (health, travel – business & private) and annual health check

  • Additional time off during Easter and Christmas

  • Monthly contribution to home internet costs and sustainable commuting

  • Annual wellness allowance to support an active lifestyle

  • Fully paid parental leave

Take the next step in your career and join us as we build the future of digital infrastructure in Norway and beyond!

Next steps

We review applicants continuously, so we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible. Deadline for your application is 5th of August. To apply, please upload your CV, Diploma and answer the questions in the application detailing your relevant experience and why you are the ideal candidate for this role.

We conduct background checks on all hires. We may also require you to obtain security clearance and a police certificate of conduct.

Bulk aim for gender balance and a diverse workforce. We do not discriminate against gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy and/or disability. We appreciate the time you have spent, and if the role aligns with your aspirations, we encourage you to apply

Because of summer vacation - you can expect feedback on your application by beginning of August.

item.title: Bulk Summer Internship 2027
item.description:
Bulk Infrastructure is building the digital backbone of the AI era. As demand for compute accelerates globally, the infrastructure layer is now among the binding constraints on AI's growth - with power, cooling, networking, and deployment capacity setting the pace of innovation. Bulk sits at the center of this transformation, developing and operating a large-scale digital infrastructure platform across the Nordics. We serve many of the world’s largest technology companies, enterprises, next-generation AI workloads, and the public and private sector in the Nordics.

We are looking for 2–4 highly motivated students to join the Bulk Summer Internship Program in the Strategy team - a small and dynamic team with experience from Private Equity, Investment Banking, Business Development, and Engineering. The internship will be based at our office in Skøyen, Oslo, and interns are expected to work from the office during the program, running two months from mid-June to mid-August 2027.

As an intern at Bulk, you will have the opportunity to work on live projects that fit your background and competencies within strategy, corporate finance, digital innovation, and sustainability. You will work closely with members of the Strategy team and present your work to the Executive Management team at Bulk Infrastructure.

Interns will also have the chance to visit at least one of our data center campuses during the summer.

Example tasks and previous internship projects:

  • Strategy: Support market intelligence work by tracking industry trends and market developments to strengthen strategic insight and decision-making

  • Corporate finance: Investment analysis on organic and/or inorganic growth opportunities

  • Digital innovation: Develop and implement solutions (e.g., AI/ML implementation) throughout the organization and/or directly in a business area

  • Product: Explore how emerging technologies could reshape future data center demand and assess the implications for infrastructure strategy

Qualifications Wishlist:

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills

  • Fast learner who thrives in fast-paced environments and is motivated by the challenge of learning something new

  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team

  • Master’s students (or students about to start a master’s degree) within business, finance, engineering, technology or equivalent - beginning on your 3rd or 4th year, fall 2026

At Bulk, we believe in the power of cross-competence teams, and the internship projects will be tailored to match candidates’ individual and combined competencies and interests.

We encourage you to apply and look forward to receiving your application by 2nd of August.


Recruitment process

  • Initial screening of applications that meet the requirements

  • Personality and ability assessments

  • Selected candidates invited to an interview, either in person or digitally

  • Final candidates are invited to an in-person Assessment Center Day at one of our data center campuses in Norway, offering insight into Bulk and the data center industry, as well as an opportunity for us to get to know each other better

Important information

  • Please apply by providing your grade transcript, CV, and cover letter through our recruitment portal

  • Please address the following topics in your cover letter: your interest in the data center industry, your motivation for this internship and for Bulk Infrastructure specifically, your most relevant skills and how you would apply them, and an example of something you have initiated independently, including motivation and outcome

  • Complete applications before deadline are required for assessment – applications missing required documentation will not be evaluated

  • References may be requested at a later stage of the process

  • Background checks will also be performed for final candidates with approval

  • You can expect feedback on your application by beginning of August


Ready to apply?

item.title: Fag-og driftsansvarlig lavspenning (LV)
item.description:
Bulk Infrastructure søker en faglig sterk elektroressurs som ønsker en rolle med høyt ansvar, stor påvirkning og en tydelig plass i kjernen av vår kritiske infrastruktur. Hos oss får du en rolle der du er tett på driften hver eneste dag. Du blir den som både forstår og forvalter regelverket, som sikrer trygg og presis gjennomføring, og som er en naturlig faglig støtte for kolleger på tvers av team. Du vil jobbe i et miljø med høy teknisk kompetanse, korte beslutningsveier og et genuint fokus på sikkerhet, kvalitet og kontinuerlig forbedring.

Bulk bygger bærekraftig infrastruktur som støtter morgendagens digitale samfunn. Vi leverer datasenterløsninger drevet av hundre prosent fornybar energi og med høye krav til oppetid, sikkerhet og driftseffektivitet. Denne rollen er avgjørende for å ivareta en trygg og forskriftsmessig drift av våre lavspenningsinstallasjoner.

Dette er en rolle for deg som ønsker å kombinere solid fagkompetanse med ansvar på vegne av eier. Du vil være den som sikrer at anlegget driftes i tråd med FSE, FEL og FEK, og at alt arbeid gjennomføres sikkert og forskriftsmessig. Samtidig er rollen operativ og tverrfaglig, og du vil jobbe tett med både drift og vedlikehold for å sikre at anlegget leverer som det skal – hver dag.

Hva vil du jobbe med?
Som fag- og driftsansvarlig lavspenning vil dine hovedoppgaver vil blant annet omfatte:

  • Ivareta det formelle driftsansvaret for lavspenningsanlegget i henhold til FSE

  • Sikre at drift skjer forsvarlig gjennom etablerte rutiner, risikovurderinger og sikkerhetstiltak

  • Være eierens representant for drift, vedlikehold og dokumentasjon av anlegget

  • Sikre at alt arbeid utføres av kvalifisert personell og i henhold til FEK og gjeldende regelverk

  • Delegere myndighet der nødvendig – og sikre at dette er tydelig dokumentert i internkontrollsystemet

  • Være teknisk eier for lavspenningssystemer (fordelinger, tavler, UPS m.m.)

  • Følge opp og kvalitetssikre forebyggende og korrigerende vedlikehold

  • Lede håndtering av feil, avvik og hendelser – inkludert rotårsaksanalyser

  • Sikre oppdatert og korrekt dokumentasjon i internkontrollsystemet

  • Godkjenne samsvarserklæringer for egne anlegg

  • Samarbeide tett med driftsteam og bidra i tekniske vurderinger ved endringer og nye installasjoner

Hvem er du?

Du er faglig trygg og trives i en rolle med tydelige krav og et stort ansvar for sikkerhet og kvalitet. Du jobber strukturert, tar gode beslutninger også når presset øker, og opptrer med naturlig faglig autoritet. Du kommuniserer klart på tvers av fagmiljøer og bygger tillit gjennom profesjonalitet, samarbeidsevne og en solid forståelse av driftsmiljøet.

For å lykkes i rollen har du:

  • Relevant bachelorgrad eller toårig fagskole med fagbrev

  • Minimum 5 års relevant praksis etter fullført utdanning

  • Oppfyller kravene i FEK §7: Bestått installatørprøve og autorisasjon, og utøvd rollen som faglig ansvarlig mist ett år de siste 10 årene

  • Solid kunnskap om FSE, FEL og FEK og gjeldende regelverk

  • Erfaring med drift, vedlikehold eller forvaltning av lavspenningsanlegg

  • Erfaring fra industri, kritisk infrastruktur, datasenter eller tilsvarende miljø

  • Gode språkferdigheter i skandinavisk og engelsk

  • Førerkort klasse B

Hvorfor denne rollen?

Dette er en rolle for deg som ønsker å jobbe med kritisk infrastruktur og ha en tydelig faglig rolle i hvordan anlegget driftes og utvikles. Du vil blant annet:

  • Ta et definert ansvar i henhold til gjeldende regelverk

  • Påvirke hvordan vi jobber med sikkerhet, kvalitet og etterlevelse

  • Jobbe tett med et kompetent og dedikert driftsteam

  • Bidra til stabil og bærekraftig drift av samfunnskritisk infrastruktur

Hva vi tilbyr våre ansatte i Bulk

  • En dynamisk og vekstorientert arbeidsplass med sterkt fokus på bærekraft

  • Gode forsikringsordninger (helse, reise – både jobb og privat) samt årlig helsesjekk

  • Ekstra fridager i påsken og julen

  • Månedlig støtte til hjemmekontor/internett og bærekraftig pendling

  • Årlig tilskudd til trening og en aktiv livsstil

  • Full lønn under foreldrepermisjon

Ta neste steg i karrieren din

Bli med oss og vær med på å bygge fremtidens digitale infrastruktur i Norge og internasjonalt.

Neste steg i prosessen

Vi vurderer kandidater fortløpende, og oppfordrer deg til å søke så snart som mulig. Endelig søknadsfrist er 03.05.2026. For å søke, vennligst last opp CV og en kort søknad hvor du beskriver din relevante erfaring og hvorfor du er en god match for rollen.

Vi gjennomfører bakgrunnssjekk ved alle ansettelser. Det kan også bli aktuelt å kreve sikkerhetsklarering og politiattest.

Bulk jobber aktivt for kjønnsbalanse og et mangfoldig arbeidsmiljø. Vi diskriminerer ikke på bakgrunn av kjønn, etnisitet, religion, seksuell orientering, alder, graviditet eller funksjonsevne. Vi setter pris på tiden du bruker på å søke, og dersom rollen treffer dine ambisjoner, håper vi å høre fra deg!

item.title: Operations Supervisor
item.description:
Are you a structured and proactive operations professional who thrives in high‑availability technical environments? Bulk Infrastructure is looking for an Operations Supervisor for our N01 site to help secure world‑class operational performance and support the digital society of tomorrow.

At Bulk, our mission is to build sustainable infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow. As one of the leading providers of sustainable, high‑performance data center solutions in the Nordics, we design and operate infrastructure that powers the digital economy – efficiently, securely, and with 100% renewable energy.

This role is central to securing stable day‑to‑day operations at N01. As Operations Supervisor, you will lead and coordinate the on‑site operational team, ensuring that critical infrastructure systems are operated within defined parameters, maintenance activities are executed smoothly, and incidents are handled effectively.

What you’ll do:

As our Operations Supervisor, your key responsibilities will be:

  • Supervise daily operations of critical infrastructure systems, including electrical, mechanical and cooling systems.

  • Ensure the site is operated within defined performance standards and operational parameters.

  • Lead, coordinate and support operations engineers and technicians, including task allocation and competence follow‑up.

  • Act as operational lead during alarms or incidents, coordinating troubleshooting, escalation and communication.

  • Ensure preventive and corrective maintenance activities are carried out according to schedule, and support contractors on site.

  • Oversee external contractors to ensure compliance with site procedures, permits, and HSE requirements.

  • Promote a strong HSE and compliance culture across all operational activities, including participation in inspections, audits, and risk assessments.

  • Maintain accurate operational logs, reports and documentation.

  • Identify and drive continuous improvements to increase efficiency, reliability and overall operational excellence.

Who are you?

You are motivated by operational excellence, safety and teamwork. You thrive in a fast‑paced environment where critical infrastructure uptime is key, and you enjoy guiding others toward high-performance execution. You bring a structured mindset and the ability to make sound decisions under pressure. Your technical insight and operational awareness help you guide others in their work and contribute to a strong, collaborative team environment.

What you bring:

  • Technical education in electrical, mechanical or similar engineering discipline.

  • Minimum 5 years of experience in technical operations, facilities management or critical infrastructure environments.

  • Hands‑on experience with electrical and mechanical systems in industrial or mission‑critical settings.

  • Experience supporting, guiding, or leading others through formal leadership roles or informal responsibility within a team

  • A solid understanding of operational processes, safety culture and critical infrastructure environments.

  • Good verbal and written communication skills in English

It is beneficial if you also have:

  • Experience from environments with high uptime requirements, such as data centres, industrial facilities, or similar

  • Familiarity with monitoring tools or systems (e.g. BMS) used in technical operations

What we offer our Bulkies:

  • A dynamic, growth-oriented workplace with a strong sustainability focus

  • Comprehensive insurance (health, travel – business & private) and annual health check

  • Additional time off during Easter and Christmas

  • Monthly contribution to home internet costs and sustainable commuting

  • Annual wellness allowance to support an active lifestyle

  • Fully paid parental leave

Take the next step in your career and join us as we build the future of digital infrastructure in Norway and beyond!

Next steps

We review applicants continuously, so we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible. The final application deadline is 18.05.2026. To apply, please upload your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are the ideal candidate for this role.

We conduct background checks on all hires. We may also require you to obtain security clearance and a police certificate of conduct.

Bulk aim for gender balance and a diverse workforce.  We do not discriminate against gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy and/or disability. We appreciate the time you have spent, and if the role aligns with your aspirations, we encourage you to apply!

item.title: Operations Engineer
item.description:
Are you a technically curious problem‑solver who enjoys operating complex systems together with a skilled team? Bulk Infrastructure is expanding our N01 campus and to support this growth we are hiring Operations Engineers across different disciplines and seniority levels. You will be part of the team that keeps our electrical, mechanical, cooling and automation systems running safely and reliably, every day.

At Bulk, our mission is to build sustainable infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow. As one of the leading providers of sustainable, high-performance data center solutions in the Nordics, we design and operate infrastructure that powers the digital economy – efficiently, securely, and with renewable energy. As an Operations Engineer, you will play a key role in securing stable operations while we continue to scale the site.

What you’ll do:

You will be part of a multidisciplinary operations team responsible for the day‑to‑day operation of electrical, mechanical, cooling and supporting systems across the N01 campus. Your responsibilities may include:

  • Operating and monitoring critical infrastructure systems such as electrical distribution, mechanical systems, cooling and HVAC, and other data centre technologies

  • Responding to alarms, deviations and operational incidents, and supporting troubleshooting activities

  • Performing preventive and corrective maintenance in line with procedures and work instructions

  • Using BMS and other monitoring platforms to identify anomalies and maintain operational stability

  • Supporting coordination of contractors and service partners on site

  • Participating in HSE activities including safety inspections, risk assessments and work permit routines

  • Maintaining accurate logs, documentation and maintenance records

  • Contributing to continuous improvement initiatives and sharing best practices across the site team

  • The role is currently daytime-based, but may transition into a shift arrangement in the future as operations grow.

Who are you?
You are organized, communicate clearly, and work well with others. You think critically, solve problems efficiently, and take initiative when needed. Above all, you prioritize safety and take responsibility for maintaining a secure, reliable environment.

What you bring:

  • Relevant technical or vocational education, such as electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, HVAC and cooling, mechatronics, industrial mechanics or a related technical field

  • Hands‑on experience from technical operations, facilities management or industrial environments

  • A strong HSE mindset and commitment to safe operations.

  • The ability to document your work, follow procedures and support risk assessments and safe work processes

  • Experience using monitoring tools such as BMS, or a willingness to learn these systems as part of your role

  • Good communication skills in English, with Scandinavian languages as an advantage

It is beneficial if you also have:

• Experience coordinating contractors or supporting maintenance activities

• Experience from critical or complex technical environments, such as data centres, energy facilities, offshore, maritime or industries operating around the clock.

Do not let a few unmet requirements hold you back from applying. If you have a unique background and believe you can make a valuable contribution to the team, we would love to hear from you.

What we offer our Bulkies:

  • A dynamic, growth-oriented workplace with a strong sustainability focus

  • Comprehensive insurance (health, travel – business & private) and annual health check

  • Additional time off during Easter and Christmas

  • Monthly contribution to home internet costs and sustainable commuting

  • Annual wellness allowance to support an active lifestyle

  • Fully paid parental leave

Take the next step in your career and join us as we build the future of digital infrastructure in Norway and beyond!

Next steps

We review applicants continuously, so we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible. To apply, please upload your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are the ideal candidate for this role.

We conduct background checks on all hires. We may also require you to obtain security clearance and a police certificate of conduct.

Bulk aim for gender balance and a diverse workforce.  We do not discriminate against gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy and/or disability. We appreciate the time you have spent, and if the role aligns with your aspirations, we encourage you to apply!

item.title: Principal Engineer Mechanical
item.description:
At Bulk, our mission is to build sustainable infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow. As one of the leading providers of sustainable, high-performance data center solutions in the Nordics, we design and operate infrastructure that powers artificial intelligence and the digital society – efficiently, securely, and with 100% renewable energy. We are now looking to hire a Principal Engineer Mechanical to play a central role in shaping the mechanical engineering standards that set the foundation for our growing data center portfolio.

The Principal Engineer Mechanical is a high-impact role with responsibility for mechanical engineering processes across multiple large-scale data center sites. You will define standards, guide site operations, and act as a key technical authority as Bulk grows. The role offers significant influence, close collaboration with senior stakeholders, and the opportunity to build a new central engineering capability from the ground up.

What you’ll do:

As our Principal Engineer Mechanical, your key responsibilities will be:

  • Lead mechanical engineering processes across Bulk’s data center portfolio (air and liquid cooling, HVAC, heat rejection, water systems, and fire systems).

  • Define and own mechanical engineering standards, policies, and procedures aligned with high-density and hyperscale requirements.

  • Perform site deep dives, audits, and health checks against design intent and target risk levels.

  • Provide capacity reviews and technical sign-off for customer deployments, including high-density and liquid-cooled workloads.

  • Lead engineering reviews and provide input to new builds.

  • Develop asset strategies aligned with ISO 55000, including criticality, maintenance plans, sparing, and lifecycle planning.

  • Act as senior escalation point during mechanical incidents, leading investigations and root cause analysis.

  • Run fleet-wide reliability and health programs using telemetry (BMS/EMS/CMMS).

  • Mentor site engineers and support capability building across the organization, including liquid cooling operations and safety.

  • Qualify vendors, approve mechanical equipment specifications, and govern deviations.

  • Support energy efficiency and sustainability initiatives, including PUE, WUE, and heat reuse strategies.

Who are you?

You are an experienced mechanical engineer with a strong understanding of mission-critical cooling systems and the credibility to act as an authority across a growing data center organization. You thrive in complex environments, enjoy setting standards from the ground up, and are comfortable balancing operational realities with long-term scalability.

What you bring:

  • Minimum of a bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering.

  • At least 10 years of experience in mission-critical facilities or data center environments.

  • Strong knowledge of cooling architectures, including CRAH/CRAC, AHUs, economization, adiabatic systems, and high-density deployments.

  • Experience with liquid cooling solutions such as direct-to-chip and rear-door heat exchangers.

  • Familiarity with ASHRAE TC 9.9 guidance and thermal management principles.

  • Experience with commissioning, performance testing, and reliability engineering (FMEA/RCFA).

  • Experience with standards such as BICSI 002, EN 50600-2-3/-4, and ISO 55000.

  • Proven experience supporting or leading incident response and root cause analysis.

  • Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to lead through influence.

Why join us?

  • A unique opportunity to shape a newly established central engineering function.

  • Influence the mechanical standards of next-generation, sustainable data centers.

  • Work at the intersection of operations, design, and future-focused infrastructure.

What we offer our Bulkies:

  • A dynamic, growth-oriented workplace with a strong sustainability focus.

  • Comprehensive insurance and annual health check.

  • Additional time off during Easter and Christmas.

  • Monthly contribution to home internet costs and sustainable commuting.

  • Annual wellness allowance to support an active lifestyle.

  • Fully paid parental leave.

Take the next step in your career and join us as we build the future of digital infrastructure in Norway and beyond!

Next steps

We are reviewing applicants continuously, so we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible. To apply, please upload your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are the ideal candidate for this role.

We conduct background checks on all hires. We may also require you to obtain security clearance and a police certificate of conduct. Bulk aim for gender balance and a diverse workforce.  We do not discriminate against gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy and/or disability. We appreciate the time you have spent, and if the role aligns with your aspirations, we encourage you to apply!

item.title: Senior Operations Manager
item.description:
At Bulk, our mission is to build sustainable infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow. As one of the leading providers of sustainable, high-performance data center solutions in the Nordics, we design and operate infrastructure that powers the digital economy – efficiently, securely, and with 100% renewable energy. As part of our continued growth, we are expanding our team and are now looking to add a new Operations Manager. This is a newly created role, established to strengthen reliable, efficient, and safe operations at our N01 Campus in Vennesla.

What you’ll do:

As our Operations Manager, your key responsibilities will be:

Operations Management

  • Lead our critical facility teams.

  • Monitor and ensure efficient operation of critical systems such as power supply, cooling, fire protection and other critical infrastructure.

  • Develop, implement and monitor plans for preventive and corrective maintenance.

  • Coordinate and supervise activities from suppliers and ensure compliance with safety and performance standards.

  • Follow up on change management and deviation handling.

Compliance and Safety

  • Ensure compliance with industry standards, local regulations and company policies.

  • Conduct regular safety audits and exercises to maintain a safe working environment.

  • Support internal and external audits.

Emergency Preparedness

  • Responsible for handling technical emergencies and coordinating rapid response strategies to minimize operational interruptions.

  • Coordinate with internal teams and external suppliers to ensure rapid resolution of issues.

Budget and Reporting

  • Manage operational budgets for the facility, including maintenance, repairs and capital improvements.

  • Track and report key indicators such as uptime, energy efficiency, maintenance and deviations.

  • Conduct incident reporting and root cause analysis for continuous improvement.

Who are you?

You are a reliable and structured leader who thrives in technically demanding environments. You value safety and enjoy solving complex challenges. With strong communication skills and a proactive mindset, you contribute to continuous improvement and secure operations.

What you bring:

  • Minimum 5 years of operational leadership experience within data centers or comparable mission‑critical environments.

  • Proven expertise in managing critical facilities, with hands‑on experience overseeing daily operations, performance, and reliability.

  • Higher education in engineering or a related technical discipline. Extensive relevant operational experience may compensate for formal education.

  • Strong technical understanding of key systems, including electrical power distribution, cooling/HVAC, fire protection, and building management systems (BMS).

  • Demonstrated commitment to HSE, with a proactive approach to safety, compliance, and continuous improvement.

  • Excellent communication skills in English; proficiency in a Scandinavian language is considered an advantage.

  • Eligibility for security clearance.

Why join us?

  • Influence how key operational processes are shaped and developed.

  • Become part of a growing organization with a strong sustainability mission.

  • Work with critical infrastructure that supports the digital society of tomorrow.

  • Collaborate with highly skilled colleagues across disciplines and locations.

What we offer our Bulkies:

  • A dynamic, growth-oriented workplace with a strong sustainability focus

  • Comprehensive insurance (health, travel – business & private) and annual health check

  • Additional time off during Easter and Christmas

  • Monthly contribution to home internet costs and sustainable commuting

  • Annual wellness allowance to support an active lifestyle

  • Fully paid parental leave

Take the next step in your career and join us as we build the future of digital infrastructure in Norway and beyond!

Next steps

We review applicants continuously, and we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. To apply, please upload your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are the ideal candidate for this role.

We conduct background checks on all hires. We may also require you to obtain security clearance and a police certificate of conduct.

Bulk aim for gender balance and a diverse workforce.  We do not discriminate against gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy and/or disability. We appreciate the time you have spent your time, and if the role aligns with your aspirations, we encourage you to apply!
